req.title: External ACH transfer
req.statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification.
preconditions: ach_link verified

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-004 defines a threshold at $10,000 for ACH transfers. Key partitions are: (1) valid transfer ≤ $10,000 with verified ACH link, (2) transfer > $10,000 without step-up yielding 422, (3) transfer with insufficient funds yielding 409, and (4) boundary values at exactly $10,000 and $10,001. State transitions cover the ACH batch queuing lifecycle. The test suite covers EP (valid partition), BVA (boundary at $10,000 and $10,001), NEG (amount > $10,000 without step-up, insufficient funds), DT (step-up required flag combinations), and STC (queued state confirmation). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up test data.

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_queues_batch
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $5,000)
ACH link is verified and active
Authentication token is valid
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=5000.00 and verified ACH account ID
trace: R-FIN-004: transfer <= $10,000 shall be accepted and ACH batch queued
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_valid_amount_queues_batch(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_customer):
    payload = {
        "from_account_id": funded_customer["account_id"],
        "to_ach_id": verified_ach_account["ach_id"],
        "amount": "5000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert body["confirmation_scheduled"] is True

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_above_limit_without_stepup_returns_422
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $15,000)
ACH link is verified and active
Authentication token is valid but lacks step-up verification claim
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=15000.00 and no step-up verification header
trace: R-FIN-004: transfers above $10,000 shall require step-up verification
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_above_limit_without_stepup_returns_422(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_customer):
    payload = {
        "from_account_id": funded_customer["account_id"],
        "to_ach_id": verified_ach_account["ach_id"],
        "amount": "15000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "STEP_UP_REQUIRED"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_at_exact_limit_accepted
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $10,000)
ACH link is verified and active
Authentication token is valid
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.00 (exactly at the limit)
trace: R-FIN-004: up to $10,000 per transfer shall be accepted
BVA upper boundary: $10,000.00 is the maximum allowed without step-up
tests.executable:

        "from_account_id": funded_customer["account_id"],
        "to_ach_id": verified_ach_account["ach_id"],
        "amount": "10000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 202
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "queued"
    assert "transfer_id" in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_requires_stepup
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $10,001)
ACH link is verified and active
Authentication token is valid but lacks step-up verification claim
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=10000.01 (one cent above the limit)
trace: R-FIN-004: transfers above $10,000 shall require step-up verification
BVA: $10,000.01 is the first value above the threshold
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_one_cent_above_limit_requires_stepup(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_customer):
    payload = {
        "from_account_id": funded_customer["account_id"],
        "to_ach_id": verified_ach_account["ach_id"],
        "amount": "10000.01",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "STEP_UP_REQUIRED"

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Customer account exists with balance of $100.00
ACH link is verified and active
Authentication token is valid
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=5000.00 against account with insufficient balance
trace: R-FIN-004: 409 on insufficient funds error pathway
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, verified_ach_account, low_balance_customer):
    payload = {
        "from_account_id": low_balance_customer["account_id"],
        "to_ach_id": verified_ach_account["ach_id"],
        "amount": "5000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    assert "transfer_id" not in body

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_above_limit_with_stepup_queues_batch
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $15,000)
ACH link is verified and active
Authentication token includes a valid step-up verification claim
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=15000.00 and step-up verification header present
trace: R-FIN-004: transfers above $10,000 WITH step-up verification shall be accepted
DT: step-up flag TRUE + amount > 10000 = accepted

tests.name: test_ach_transfer_state_transitions_queued_to_processing
preconditions: Customer account exists with sufficient balance (>= $1,000)
ACH link is verified and active
Authentication token is valid
actions: POST /transfers/ach with amount=1000.00 to initiate transfer
expected: Status does not regress from 'processing' back to 'queued'
trace: R-FIN-004: ACH batch queued on successful submission
STC: queued -> processing state transition is valid
tests.executable:
def test_ach_transfer_state_transitions_queued_to_processing(api_client, verified_ach_account, funded_customer):
    payload = {
        "from_account_id": funded_customer["account_id"],
        "to_ach_id": verified_ach_account["ach_id"],
        "amount": "1000.00",
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    post_resp = api_client.post("/transfers/ach", json=payload)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 202
    transfer_id = post_resp.json()["transfer_id"]
    assert post_resp.json()["status"] == "queued"
    get_resp = api_client.get(f"/transfers/ach/{transfer_id}")
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    valid_states = {"queued", "processing", "completed"}
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] in valid_states
